about.title: About Me
description: Hey! I'm a gameplay programmer with over two years of experience. I focus on building solid and flexible architecture, with a strong interest in AI behaviors, gameplay systems, and complex mechanics.

I've always been drawn to games that reward curiosity and skill, and I try to bring that same mindset into my work. For me, programming isn't just about making things work, it's about helping the player feel something through how the game responds.

blocks.title: Project
blocks.text: AR/VR multiplayer platformer developed for Oculus, awarded the Lion at the Biennale di Venezia (2022).

blocks.title: Key Contributions
items: Built an enemy AI system using a finite state machine with behavior trees, so enemies could react to each other and suport multiple states such as idle, chase and attack.
Built core player mechanics including wall-climbing parkour and a combo-based combat system with input-driven attacks, mid-air moves, and chained abilities.
Worked on an authoritative server multiplayer model, making sure that attacks, pickups and throwing items felt right even under network lag.
Developed gameplay systems specifically for VR, working hands-on with Oculus headsets and integrating input and interaction using the XR Toolkit.

blocks.title: Summary
blocks.text: Developed a 3D First Person Speedrunnign game from scratch in Unity, building custom editor tools to make the development process easier. Also designed and made websites from scratch using JavaScript, HTML5, CSS and JSON, including this portfolio.

blocks.title: Summary
blocks.text: Early stage development of a mobile platform where companies can market their own products throug minigames. Currently working on the first prototype, covering gamplay programming and making the game customizable for the clients.

blocks.title: Responsibilities
items: Programming a mobile minigame using Unity.
Build the game in a way that is easy to customize for different clients.
Implementing UI, fucusing on clarity and responsiveness across different mobile screen sizes.

blocks.title: About the Game
blocks.text: Rush Rift is a fast-paced first-person speedrunner game where every second counts. Dash through cyberpunk arenas, chain attacks with precision, and keep your momentum alive as you race toward the finish.

blocks.title: My Role
blocks.text: As Lead Programmer, I developed the core movement, dash, and attack systems, as well as the additive level-loading architecture, focusing on scalability, performance, and clean modular code in Unity.

blocks.title: About the Game
blocks.text: A personal project focused on AI development and combat mechanics, created to experiment with system design and gameplay feel.

blocks.title: My Role
blocks.text: Developed independently from start to finish to strengthen my technical and design skills in Unity.

blocks.title: About the Game
text: Gun Bandit is a 2D top-down shooter/puzzle game where you play as a mercenary hired to carry out dangerous missions inspired in Hotline Miami.
Play your way, go in guns blazing, or take a stealthy approach and avoid detection. Every mission is a choice: fight or sneak.
Simple prototype to demonstrate pathfinding, state machine, and enemy AI.

blocks.title: My Role
blocks.text: As Lead Programmer, In this project I was the sole programmer, I was responsible for programming the player, weapon system, enemies and UI.

blocks.title: About the Game
text: In 3047 you play as a space bounty hunter, where you were task to eliminate groups of bandits and save the galaxy.
It is a 3D shoot em up, until the boss battle starts, and it becomes a bullet hell.

blocks.title: My Role
blocks.text: I coded the player, damage system, UI, animations, enemies and boss behaviour.

designPatterns.title: Design patterns
designPatterns.desc: Factory, Builder, Command, Mediator, Strategy, Observer, Object Pooling

aiAlgorithms.title: AI algorithms
aiAlgorithms.desc: A*, Dijkstra, FSM, Steering Behaviors, Obstacle Avoidance, Roulette, Flocking, Decision Trees, Behavior Trees
